summary.notes: Plugin has 125 PHP files, 13k LOC. One taint flow was flagged (xss-001). Manual trace shows it is a false positive. All other output in admin views uses proper WP escaping functions. Feed templates use esc_xml/esc_url throughout. No SQLi, path traversal, CSRF, or auth bypass issues found.

verdicts.original_claim: get_option('xmlsf_news_tags') tainted value flows through wp_terms_checklist() into echo $cat_list without escaping.

why_false_positive: wp_terms_checklist() is a WordPress core function (wp-admin/includes/template.php) that uses Walker_Category_Checklist internally.
Walker_Category_Checklist::start_el() calls esc_html() on term names and esc_attr() on IDs and other attributes before generating HTML.
The function is designed to return pre-escaped HTML safe for direct output — this is its documented usage pattern.
The $selected_categories array contains only integer category IDs (from saved DB option), which are safely used as checked= comparisons, not rendered as HTML.
The str_replace() only substitutes a fixed attribute name string, introducing no attacker-controlled content.
The phpcs:ignore comment (WordPress.Security.EscapeOutput.OutputNotEscaped) is appropriate here because PHPCS cannot introspect core function return values.
To inject a malicious category name an attacker would need administrator access to create/edit taxonomy terms — at which point they already have full site control.
Reviewed analogous pattern: WordPress's own post editor echoes wp_terms_checklist() output directly in edit-form-advanced.php without additional escaping.

areas_audited: inc/class-sitemap-news.php - request filtering, SQL WHERE clause (safe via sanitize_number)
views/admin/*.php - output escaping audit (all use esc_html/esc_attr/esc_url)
